name: Secure API Service
description: Security-first AGENTS.md for backend API services with strict validation, secrets management, and quality gates.
content:
# AGENTS.md

> This file defines the rules and expectations for AI agents working in this secure API service repository.

---

## Table of Contents

- [Backend API Design Standards](#backend-api-design-standards)
- [Error Handling & Logging](#error-handling--logging)
- [Database Interaction Guidelines](#database-interaction-guidelines)
- [Input Validation & Output Encoding](#input-validation--output-encoding)
- [Secrets Management](#secrets-management)
- [Prompt Injection Prevention](#prompt-injection-prevention)
- [Testing Strategy](#testing-strategy)
- [Coverage Requirements](#coverage-requirements)
- [Linting & Formatting Tools](#linting--formatting-tools)
- [Code Style Rules](#code-style-rules)
- [Lint Suppression Policy](#lint-suppression-policy)

---

## Backend API Design Standards

All endpoints follow REST conventions with `/api/v1/` prefix. Resource names are plural nouns. Correct HTTP methods and status codes are mandatory. All error responses use the standard envelope with `code`, `message`, `details`, and `requestId`. Pagination uses cursor-based approach.

---

## Error Handling & Logging

Never swallow errors. Centralise error handling in middleware. Include `requestId` in all responses and logs. Never expose stack traces in production. Use structured JSON logging. Do not log PII.

---

## Database Interaction Guidelines

Parameterised queries only — no SQL concatenation. Wrap multi-step operations in transactions. All schema changes are versioned migrations with `up` and `down` steps. Use connection pools with explicit configuration.

---

## Input Validation & Output Encoding

Validate all inputs at the API boundary using a schema library (zod, joi, pydantic). Allowlist permitted values. Enforce string length limits and numeric bounds on every field. Encode outputs for their context (HTML, SQL, shell, URL).

---

## Secrets Management

No hardcoded secrets in source code — ever. Use a managed secrets service. Run `gitleaks` pre-commit. Enable push protection. Document rotation procedures. Rotate on employee departure or suspected compromise.

---

## Prompt Injection Prevention

For any LLM-integrated endpoints: treat all LLM output as untrusted, sanitise inputs before prompt inclusion, use structured outputs with schema validation, separate user data from instructions using delimiters, and log all LLM interactions. The LLM operates with minimum privilege — no direct database or file system access.

---

## Testing Strategy

70% unit, 20% integration, 10% E2E. Every new function requires a test. Write tests in the same PR. Use Jest (Node) or pytest (Python). Integration tests use real containerised dependencies via testcontainers.

---

## Coverage Requirements

Minimum 80% line, 75% branch, 80% function coverage. Blocking CI thresholds. A PR that drops coverage by more than 2% requires explicit justification.

---

## Linting & Formatting Tools

JavaScript/TypeScript: ESLint + Prettier. Python: Ruff + Black. Pre-commit hooks enforce formatting. Linting failures block merge. CI runs `prettier --check` (not `--write`).

---

## Code Style Rules

TypeScript strict mode. No `any` without justified suppression. No `var`. `const` by default. Maximum function length 50 lines. Cyclomatic complexity ≤ 10. Imports sorted: external, internal, relative.

---

## Lint Suppression Policy

Minimum scope, specific rule name, justification comment required. Never suppress security rules. All suppressions reviewed in PR. Track total suppression count — alert on > 10% growth per PR.
